name: Navalperal de Pinares y Hoyo de Pinares — circular desde Cebreros
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cebreros traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes, offering challenging climbs and descents. The region features a mix of pine forests, open countryside, and routes that pass near reservoirs such as Embalse de San Juan and El Burguillo. Mountain passes like Puerto de Arrebatacapas and Puerto de Navalmoral are notable features, providing varied terrain for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Cebreros The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de San Juan y Estación de Cebreros (DSA 2) — circular desde Cebreros, a 40.4 miles (65.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain as it circles the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Navalperal de Pinares y Hoyo de Pinares — circular desde Cebreros, a difficult 45.8 miles (73.8 km) path. This route leads through areas with pine forests and presents significant climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Arrebatacapas – Puerto de Navalmoral loop from Cebreros, a 65.2 miles (104.9 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 5 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Cebreros is defined by challenging mountain passes, scenic reservoirs, and stretches through pine forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Cebreros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 2,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cebreros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cebreros?
A: Cebreros offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 80 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling in Cebreros?
A: Road cycling in Cebreros is characterized by significant elevation changes, including challenging climbs and descents. The region features a mix of moderate and difficult routes, with 44 difficult and 37 moderate routes, alongside a few easier options.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cebreros?
A: While many routes in Cebreros feature significant elevation, there are 6 easy road cycling routes available. These provide a good starting point for those new to road cycling in the area or looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Cebreros?
A: The terrain around Cebreros is diverse, featuring pine forests, open countryside, and routes that pass near reservoirs like Embalse de San Juan and El Burguillo. You'll also encounter mountain passes such as Puerto de Arrebatacapas and Puerto de Navalmoral, offering varied and often challenging landscapes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Cebreros?
A: Yes, Cebreros offers several long-distance routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatacapas – Puerto de Navalmoral loop from Cebreros&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 65.2-mile (104.9 km) trail that takes you through mountain passes.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by beautiful natural and man-made attractions. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2788211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other points of interest in the wider area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3441641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navahonda Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3886809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrid Deep Space Communications Complex&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Cebreros?
A: Yes, many routes in Cebreros are designed as circular loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063098749/embalse-de-san-juan-y-estacion-de-cebreros-dsa-2-circular-desde-cebreros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de San Juan y Estación de Cebreros (DSA 2) — circular desde Cebreros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.4 miles (65.0 km) and circles the reservoir.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cebreros?
A: The road cycling routes in Cebreros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, scenic reservoirs, and the diverse stretches through pine forests.

Q: Which route offers a good balance of challenge and scenic beauty around reservoirs?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31932207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burguillo Reservoir – Burguillo Reservoir loop from La Chorreta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 71.8 km (44.6 miles) route that offers beautiful views of the reservoir and a good challenge without being overly difficult.

Q: Are there routes that explore the pine forests of the region?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063110600/navalperal-de-pinares-y-hoyo-de-pinares-circular-desde-cebreros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares y Hoyo de Pinares — circular desde Cebreros&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.8-mile (73.8 km) route that leads through areas rich in pine forests and includes significant climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cebreros?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and potential for heat, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Cebreros. Summer can be quite warm, especially on exposed sections, while winter may bring colder temperatures to the mountain passes.

Q: Are there any routes that feature notable climbs?
A: Many routes in Cebreros are known for their challenging climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Arrebatacapas – Puerto de Navalmoral loop from Cebreros&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes substantial ascents over mountain passes, making it a favorite for those seeking a demanding ride.
